self_assessment.weakest_area: Severity collapse to 3 buckets means critical and serious now share the error color. Previously they were red vs orange — distinct. The new split may feel less graded on the accessibility severity breakdown bar. Verified via screenshot that the ordered rows still read correctly but the visual distinction between critical/serious is weaker than before.
self_assessment.hardest_decision: Keeping the social-preview cards in seo.vue with raw brand Tailwind colors. Those 21 remaining violations are intentional platform mimicry, not design-system violations. Documented as an exception in the guidelines Avoid section and Design Decisions.

known_limitations: Scan history requires a real scan to render meaningfully. /results/* routes were not exercised in the browser verify pass (need a scanId).
Strict AA contrast for text-dimmed at sub-14px is still open (prior review PARTIAL item).
Warnings during pnpm dev: 'esbuild option was specified by vite:vue-jsx plugin' — upstream, not related.
next_steps: Run a real scan and visually verify /results/[scanId]/{accessibility,performance,best-practices,seo,scan} render cleanly with new semantic tokens.
If critical/serious visual distinction is too weak after use, reconsider spreading across 4 shades (critical=error-solid, serious=error-soft, moderate=warning, minor+info=info).
